column5: Strategic alignment- aligning growth and margin strategy to needs
column14: How explicitly does AI enhance the customer value proposition (e.g., personalization, NPS, retention) and tie to growth/margin outcomes?
column15: No explicit linkage between AI initiatives and customer value; no owned metrics; impact anecdotes only.
column16: Some qualitative links to CX (e.g., personalization pilots); limited metrics on NPS/retention; isolated case studies.
column17: Defined hypotheses and KPIs (e.g., NPS, churn, ARPU); initial A/B tests show localized gains in ≥1 segment/market.
column18: Programmatic measurement across ≥3 segments/markets; uplift targets embedded in product/CRM plans; ≥2 validated metrics (e.g., +NPS, −churn).
column19: AI‑driven value creation is a core strategy lever; multi‑segment, multi‑market gains sustained (e.g., NPS +10, churn −15% YoY); outcomes drive roadmap & pricing.

column5: Strategic alignment- aligning growth and margin strategy to needs
column14: Evidence that AI strategy targets disruptive differentiation (new AI‑native offerings, pricing models, or channels) beyond efficiency.
column15: Efficiency‑only narrative; no AI‑native business lines, products, or services or monetization ideas.
column16: One‑off ideation sessions; early concepts exist but no validation pipeline.
column17: Disruption opportunities captured in a living backlog; discovery experiments run quarterly; ≥1 concept with customer validation.
column18: Dedicated AI venture/innovation track; staged funding and gates; ≥2 AI‑native offerings in pilot with unit economics tracked. (products/services/business lines)
column19: AI‑native businesses, products, business lines operating at scale; cannibalization managed; disruptive revenue ≥10% of digital revenue with clear growth trajectory.

column5: Trust and change management- secure stakeholder buy-in to drive change
column14: How clear are cross‑functional roles and responsibilities for AI (business, product, data, risk, legal) from idea to run?

column16: Some role definitions exist (e.g., product/data); risk/legal engaged late.
column17: RACI defined for build/deploy/run; governance touchpoints scheduled; risk/legal involved pre‑deployment on sensitive use cases.
column18: End‑to‑end operating model codified; stage gates with accountable owners; coverage ≥80% of AI use cases.
column19: Operating model measured and improved; role clarity audited; >95% of use cases follow defined RACI with SLA adherence.

column5: Continuous learning and improvement. Refine models with new attributes and data

column18: Targets set vs top‑quartile peers; OKRs tied to gap closure; retrospectives drive actions; measurable YoY improvement.
column19: Continuous benchmarking with automated data feeds; OKRs auto‑updated; sustained top‑quartile performance across ≥4 core metrics.

column5: Business deployment- integration into on-going operations

column19: Enterprise telemetry embedded; 100% critical models covered; explainability, audit trails, and rollback integrated; exec reviews monthly.

column14: Is there a standardized, auditable methodology to estimate and realize ROI for AI (ex‑ante and ex‑post)?

column18: ROI approach audited; causal methods (e.g., A/B, diff‑in‑diff); ≥75% launches with verified ex‑post impact; benefits booked in finance.
column19: Portfolio‑level ROI tracked quarterly; forecast vs actual variance ≤10%; ROI learnings feed selection and funding decisions.

column14: How well is delivery aligned with applicable AI/sector regulations during rollout and operation (e.g., DPIA, model cards, DSARs)?

column19: Compliance‑by‑design; obligations monitored continuously; audits passed; evidence generated automatically; zero major findings in last 12 months.

column5: Business deployment- integration into on-going operations

column19: Dynamic rebalancing on signal changes; consistent horizon mix achieved; kill rates transparent; value/throughput steadily improves.
